title: Quantitative scanning thermal microscopy studies of the influence of interfaces and heat transport anisotropy in 2D materials
journal: Proceedings of the Microscience Microscopy Congress 2023 incorporating EMAG 2023
type1_summary: No figure-ready cleaned data files were provided in the inspected file list.
type2_summary: No raw measurement files were provided in the inspected file list.
classification_notes: The paper itself reports quantitative HV-SThM measurements and extracted thermal parameters, but the provided file inventory contains no actual data tables, source data, or raw instrument outputs. Therefore no Type 1 or Type 2 dataset file can be identified from the available files.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper text describes the study and figures, but no explicit data repository, source-data package, or downloadable dataset is mentioned in the provided publication summary.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Single PDF article text file from Zenodo, 320.4 KB, which is consistent with a paper/manuscript document rather than a data table or raw measurement file.
file_classifications.reasoning: This file is the article itself, not a dataset file containing measurements. It serves as documentation for the study and provides context for the experimental methods and results, but it is not replot-ready data.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies heat transport in 2D materials using quantitative high-vacuum scanning thermal microscopy (HV-SThM), focusing on anisotropic graphene, γ-InSe, and perovskite flakes on Si and SiO2 substrates. The main findings reported are that interface thermal resistance affects heat transport only up to a certain limit, after which it becomes negligible, and that the method can resolve nanoscale variations in thermal properties and quantify anisotropic thermal conductivities and interfacial thermal resistivity.

figures.description: Schematic of the HV-SThM setup showing the probe, sample, laser/topographic feedback, photodiode, Wheatstone bridge, and variable resistance used to measure probe temperature changes during scanning.
Fabrication workflow for preparing substrates and 2D flakes, including exfoliation and BEXP, solvent treatment, Ar ions, Ar/O2 plasma, and formation of wedged flakes.
Summary of materials and extracted thermal parameters, listing interfacial thermal resistance (rint) and thermal conductivities kxy and kz for γ-InSe/Si and perovskite/SiO2.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The raw measurements are HV-SThM scans collected under high vacuum at room temperature while moving a resistively heated probe across the sample surface. The paper indicates that the probe temperature changes with probe-sample heat flow and that quantitative maps of sample heat transport are obtained from these scans.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: No explicit data availability statement, repository link, downloadable dataset, or code availability statement is present in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are not explicitly described as files or formats. The paper only states that HV-SThM measurements were performed and that the probe was scanned across the sample surface to obtain thermal maps.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data appear as extracted quantitative values of interfacial thermal resistance and anisotropic thermal conductivities summarized in a table for specific material/substrate systems.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-level data are partially present only as a summarized table of extracted parameters; no explicit source-data files or per-figure downloadable datasets are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The text is a short conference-style paper with schematic figures and a results table rather than a full data paper. The dataset semantics are therefore limited: the main experimental output is spatially resolved thermal maps from HV-SThM, but only derived quantitative parameters are explicitly shown in the provided text. No supplementary data, raw scan files, or repository information is given.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper describes direct HV-SThM measurements: the probe is scanned across the sample surface and Vout changes with probe-sample heat flow, producing quantitative maps of heat transport.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: A table reports extracted values of rint, kxy, and kz for specific samples, indicating processed/figure-ready quantitative data.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: No explicit data availability statement is included in the provided text.
